verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 encodes HSP60, a mitochondrial chaperonin protein. The NCBI summary explicitly identifies it as a mitochondrial protein with functions in mitochondrial protein folding and assembly. HGNC gene-group memberships confirm mitochondrial nucleoid association. While the summary mentions a potential role as a signaling molecule in innate immunity, this does not establish surface accessibility of the protein body itself. The protein is localized to the mitochondrial matrix/inner compartment, not the plasma membrane. No membrane-anchored isoform, GPI anchor, or surface trafficking mechanism is documented. The protein does not reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane by its own mechanism.
